commit.message:
ci: Support multi-branch Docker image builds (dev, backup branches)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: comprehensive unit test coverage + bug fixes (#37)

* test: comprehensive unit test coverage for all modules

- Add tests for logger, graph/node, executor (extended), spec,
  assessor, health, prompts, monitor/detect, and monitor/loop
- Fix duplicate _accept_bot_invitation definition in cli.py (merge artifact)
- Fix _restore_from_store not writing abandoned→pending back to bead store
- Fix plan node expansion using NodeResult instead of ExecutionResult
- Fix AttributeError not caught in monitor/detect conflict PR check
- Fix lstrip("mod:") → removeprefix("mod:") in assessor module classification
- Update test_cli_bot.py for explicit token signature on _accept_bot_invitation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(plan): increase max_tokens from 1500 to 4096 to prevent truncated JSON

Larger specs (e.g. option-forge) produced plan JSON exceeding 1500 tokens,
causing unterminated string parse errors. 4096 gives headroom for complex plans.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): warn and prompt when spec project doesn't match target repo

Prevents accidentally running a spec against the wrong repo by comparing
spec.project against the repo name slug. Prompts "Continue anyway? [y/N]"
so the user can abort before any issues are filed or beads written.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(plan): skip readme/docs modules when emitting build nodes

The executor always emits a dedicated readme node post-merge.
If the plan LLM includes a "readme" or "docs" module, it would
create a redundant impl issue. Filter it out at build-node emit time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(plan): inter-module build dependencies via module_dependencies

PlanArtifact gains a module_dependencies field. The plan LLM now outputs
which modules must be fully merged before another can build. _emit_build_nodes
wires depends_on to the upstream merge node IDs so the DAG executor enforces
the order — integration-test modules always build last.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(status): order graph nodes by type (plan→research→build→merge→readme)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(prompts): require full test suite run and regression triage in build agent

Agents were running only their new tests and missing pre-existing failures
and regressions. Now explicitly: run `uv run pytest` (full suite), stash-check
if failures are pre-existing, fix regressions, note pre-existing failures in PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(prompts): run full-tree lint and format check, not just new files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(executor): add wont-do node state for intentional permanent skips

- abandoned = failed, will be retried on re-run
- wont-do   = intentionally skipped, never re-dispatched even on restart

_restore_from_store now keeps wont-do nodes permanent and resets abandoned
nodes to pending so re-runs act as repairs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove pycache files from git tracking

__pycache__ and .pyc files were tracked in git despite being in .gitignore.
Untrack them so they stop appearing in PRs and diffs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Track E: Prove buildFseTable structural invariants — table size and accuracy log passthrough (#718)

* feat: prove Zstd value decoding bounds (match length ≥ 3, offset ≥ 2)

Add two characterizing property theorems for Zstd sequence value decoding
(RFC 8878 §3.1.1.3.2.1.2 and §3.1.1.4):

- `decodeMatchLenValue_ge_three`: any successful match length decoding
  produces a value ≥ 3, the Zstd minimum match length. Uses `decide_cbv`
  on a Fin-bounded proposition to verify all 53 table baselines are ≥ 3.

- `decodeOffsetValue_ge_two`: non-repeat offset codes (code ≥ 1) produce
  values ≥ 2, distinguishing them from repeat offsets. Follows from
  2^code ≥ 2 when code ≥ 1.

Closes #698

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for value decoding bounds session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add GetElem vs getInternal omega mismatch and decide_cbv patterns to array skill

Documents two related patterns discovered during value decoding bounds proofs:
1. omega can't unify GetElem-based and getInternal-based array access after unfold;
   use exact with Nat.le_trans instead
2. decide_cbv on Fin-bounded propositions for verifying all entries of concrete
   arrays, with Nat-indexed wrapper to avoid Fin coercion issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Review: Close stale PR #689 and audit ZstdSequence spec composability (#699)

* doc: add WF skill guidance on do-notation losing dependent if hypotheses

Documents that `if h : cond then throw` in `do` blocks does NOT preserve
the negated hypothesis through monadic bindings. The fix is to use
explicit `if ... then .error ... else do ...` instead. This pattern
was encountered during the decompressBlocksWF refactoring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: review ZstdSequence spec composability and close stale PR #689

Audit all 25 theorems in Zip/Spec/ZstdSequence.lean for signature
compatibility toward future executeSequences correctness proofs.
Found 4 signature gaps, the most significant being that ValidSequence
constrains raw offsets while the loop checks resolved offsets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: prove buildFseTable_cells_size (table size = 1 <<< accuracyLog)

Prove that when buildFseTable succeeds, the returned cells array has
size exactly 1 <<< accuracyLog. The proof works by:

1. Decomposing the do-notation bind chain (Except.bind_eq_ok') to
   extract individual loop equations
2. Threading a cells-size invariant through all 4 forIn loops using
   forIn_range_preserves, which bridges from Std.Legacy.Range.forIn
   to List.forIn'.loop via forIn_eq_forIn_range'
3. Showing each loop body preserves cells size (Array.set! preserves
   size, identity branches preserve size)

Also adds three reusable helper lemmas:
- Except.bind_eq_ok': decompose successful bind chains
- forIn'_loop_preserves: thread invariants through List.forIn'.loop
- forIn_range_preserves: bridge from range forIn to loop invariants

Closes #610

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Add Discord-style startup splash with auto-update check

App now shows a splash screen on launch:
1. "시작 중..." → "업데이트 확인 중..." with spinner
2. If update found: download with progress bar and percentage
3. Windows: auto-apply + restart, macOS: open DMG
4. Then proceed to license check → dashboard

Only checks for updates in frozen (PyInstaller) builds.
10-second timeout on update check, graceful failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Handle neutral site games for conference/NCAA tournaments

The ESPN API returns neutralSite=true for tournament games played at
a third venue. Previously the model always applied a +3.5pt home court
advantage to whoever ESPN labeled 'home', which is wrong for neutral sites.

Changes:
- _parse_event(): extract neutralSite flag from ESPN competition data
- analyze_game(): pass is_home=0 to build_feature_vector when neutral_site=True
  (removes the +3.5pt HCA from the feature vector)
- app.py scan loop: pass is_home=False for home team at neutral sites so
  home/road win-pct splits are not applied (use overall record instead)

Profil dropdown dans header + fix auth redirect loop

- L'etudiant peut voir et modifier son profil depuis le dropdown

- Fix logout: redirect explicite vers index.html apres signOut
- Fix soumission: utilise userProfile au lieu des champs supprimes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hearth: Live Activity panel expands downward pushing other panels off screen (Forge-zx5) (#41)

* fix: bound Workers panel height to prevent pushing other panels off screen

The renderWorkers function joins two bordered sub-panels vertically, but
didn't account for each sub-panel's border adding 2 lines. This made the
combined output 2 lines taller than sibling single-panel columns, causing
the Live Activity panel to expand downward and push Queue/Event panels
off screen.

Fix: deduct 2 from the available content height before splitting between
the worker list and activity sub-panels, and add a MaxHeight clamp on the
combined output as a safety net.

Bead: Forge-zx5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-zx5

- Clamp Workers panel MaxHeight to `height` (not `height+2`) to match
  sibling panels' height budget
- Trim trailing newlines before counting lines in test assertion
- Rename test to TestRenderWorkersDoesNotExceedSinglePanel to reflect
  the overflow-guard assertion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-zx5

- Remove MaxHeight clamp in renderWorkers to avoid truncating borders
- Tighten test assertion to require equal line counts between panels

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
remove pipes from encoded output and add CLI

- format_encoded now uses space-separated output instead of pipe delimiters
- add cli.py with bookcrypt console script entry point
- update pyproject.toml to register CLI entry point
- update tests to match new output format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix poison wait false positives and alert log parsing (#445) (#447)

* Fix #410 (#411)

* Fix #412 (#413)

* GUI installer: log installation history to config.installation_history (#414)

The GUI installer read from installation_history for version detection but
never wrote to it, causing subsequent upgrades to fail to detect the prior
install. Adds LogInstallationHistoryAsync mirroring the CLI installer's
existing LogInstallationHistory method.

Fixes #409

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Feature/long running queries config settings (#415)

* Added exclusions in GetLongRunningQueriesAsync() method for SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTIC wait types, and WAITFOR wait types.

* Added TOP parameter for query in GetLongRunningQueriesAsync() method to allow future configurability on the number of long-running queries returned.  Added optional parameter to control display of WAITFOR types in future.

* Replaced waitForFilter string constructor with C# string interpolation instead of janky string addition.

* Added exclusion for backup-related waits to GetLongRunningQueriesAsync() method.  Removed System.Collections.Generic using statement as it is unnecessary.

* Reverted Controls/LandingPage.xaml.cs

* Added BROKER_RECEIVE_WAITFOR wait type to waitforFilter exclusions.  Added miscWaitsFilter to exclude XE_LIVE_TARGET_TVF waits.  Removed unused parameters.  Corrected minimum value for maxLongRunningQueryCount (minimum 1 instead of 5).

* Added filtering for SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS, WAITFOR, BROKER_RECEIVE_WAITFOR, BACKUPTHREAD, BACKUPIO, and XE_LIVE_TARGET_TVF wait types in Lite/Services/LocalDataService.WaitStats.cs

* Add configurable max results setting for long-running queries

Adds LongRunningQueryMaxResults to UserPreferences (default 5) and
exposes it in the Settings UI alongside the existing duration threshold.
Threads the value through GetAlertHealthAsync and GetLongRunningQueriesAsync
to replace the hardcoded TOP(5) in the DMV query.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply max results validation and Lite parity for long-running queries

Adds Math.Clamp(1, int.MaxValue) guard to GetLongRunningQueriesAsync in
both Dashboard and Lite, and updates the Dashboard settings validation
to use an explicit range check with a descriptive error message.

Mirrors the LongRunningQueryMaxResults setting across the Lite project:
adds the App property, loads/saves it to settings.json, exposes it in
the Lite Settings UI, and passes it through to GetLongRunningQueriesAsync
to replace the hardcoded LIMIT 5.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use GetInt64() when loading long-running query max results from JSON

Prevents an OverflowException if the value in settings.json is outside
the int32 range. The value is read as long, clamped to [1, int.MaxValue],
then cast back to int.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add configurable long-running query filter toggles

Replaces hardcoded wait type exclusions in GetLongRunningQueriesAsync
with user-configurable booleans for SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS, WAITFOR /
BROKER_RECEIVE_WAITFOR, backup waits, and miscellaneous waits. All
four filters default to true (existing behavior preserved). Settings
are exposed in the Notifications section of both Dashboard and Lite
Settings UIs and persisted to UserPreferences / settings.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* merged with incoming dev branch

* Parameterized TOP/LIMIT value in Dashboard/Services/DatabaseService.NocHealth.cs and Lite/Services/LocalDataService.WaitStats.cs, and clamped the upper bound of the value to 1000 to avoid foot shooting.  Removed blank lines as per Erik's request.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Sync plan viewer fixes from plan-b: spool labels, unmatched index detail (#416)

- Spool operators now show Eager/Lazy prefix (e.g., "Eager Index Spool"
  instead of just "Index Spool") by prepending from LogicalOp
- PlanIconMapper entries added for all Eager/Lazy spool variants
- UnmatchedIndexes warning now parses child Parameterization elements
  to show specific database.schema.table.index names

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fixes many warnings, and pre-calculates the RegEx patterns at compile time instead of at runtime: warning CA1847: Use 'string.Contains(char)' instead of 'string.Contains(string)' when searching for a single character (https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/fundamentals/code-analysis/quality-rules/ca1847) warning CA1875: Use 'Regex.Count' instead of 'Regex.Matches(...).Count' (https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/fundamentals/code-analysis/quality-rules/ca1875) warning CA1863: Cache a 'CompositeFormat' for repeated use in this formatting operation (https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/fundamentals/code-analysis/quality-rules/ca1863) (#346)

Co-authored-by: Orestes Zoupanos <orestes.zoupanos@tpr.gov.uk>

* Complete GeneratedRegex conversion and remove Compiled flags (#420)

PR #346 left Lite PlanAnalyzer with 3 old-style regex fields and inline
Regex.IsMatch calls, and added unnecessary RegexOptions.Compiled to all
GeneratedRegex attributes (source generator always compiles, flag is ignored).

- Convert remaining Lite regex fields to use GeneratedRegex source generator
- Convert all inline Regex.IsMatch calls to GeneratedRegex in both apps
- Remove RegexOptions.Compiled from all [GeneratedRegex] attributes
- Keep both PlanAnalyzer copies in sync with identical regex methods

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add permissions section to README with least-privilege setup (#421)

Documents required permissions for all platforms: on-prem Full/Lite,
Azure SQL Database (contained user), Azure SQL MI, and AWS RDS.
Includes copy-paste SQL scripts. Updates comparison table and
troubleshooting to link to the new section.

Prompted by user question in issue #418.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace custom TrayToolTip with plain ToolTipText to fix crash

The custom visual TrayToolTip (Border + TextBlock) triggers a known race
condition in Hardcodet.NotifyIcon.Wpf where Popup.CreateWindow throws
"The root Visual of a VisualTarget cannot have a parent." This crash
poisons the ReaderWriterLockSlim on the UI thread, breaking Overview
queries permanently until restart.

Plain string ToolTipText avoids the WPF Popup infrastructure entirely.

Fixes #422

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add resilience to DuckDB read lock acquisition

If an unhandled exception leaks a read lock on the UI thread,
subsequent EnterReadLock() calls throw LockRecursionException
permanently. Catch this and return a no-op disposable instead,
since the thread already has read protection in place.

This makes the lock self-healing: even if a crash leaks the lock,
subsequent operations recover gracefully rather than failing forever.

Fixes #423

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Restore custom TrayToolTip and silently handle Hardcodet crash (issue #422)

Reverts the ToolTipText approach which caused context menu positioning and
theme regressions. Instead, keeps the original custom TrayToolTip for proper
dark theme styling and popup anchoring, and adds IsTrayToolTipCrash() detection
in both apps' DispatcherUnhandledException handlers to silently swallow the
rare Hardcodet VisualTarget race condition without showing error dialogs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix incorrect table name in Data Retention section

README referenced config.retention_settings which doesn't exist.
Retention is configured via the retention_days column in
config.collection_schedule. Same bug reported in #223 but the
fix never actually landed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix RID Lookup analyzer rule to match new PhysicalOp label (#429)

The Key Lookup parser fix (PR #413) changes PhysicalOp from
"RID Lookup" to "RID Lookup (Heap)". The analyzer rule used exact
equality which no longer matched. Changed to StartsWith.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add uninstall option to CLI and GUI installers (#431)

Adds complete uninstall capability that removes all server-level objects:
- 3 SQL Agent jobs (Collection, Data Retention, Hung Job Monitor)
- 2 Extended Events sessions (BlockedProcess, Deadlock)
- Server-side traces
- PerformanceMonitor database

Changes:
- New install/00_uninstall.sql standalone script for SSMS users
- CLI: --uninstall flag with interactive confirmation
- GUI: Uninstall button (red, enabled when installed version detected)
- Fix: existing clean install now removes all 3 jobs + XE sessions
  (previously missed Hung Job Monitor and both XE sessions)

blocked process threshold (s) is intentionally NOT reset during uninstall
as other monitoring tools may depend on it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* LOB compression + deduplication for query stats tables (#419)

Databases were growing to 150-200 GB in under a week on busy servers.
LOB columns (query_text, query_plan_text, query_sql_text, compilation_metrics)
were 92-94% of storage. COMPRESS()/DECOMPRESS() achieves 90-91% reduction.

Schema changes:
- query_stats, query_store_data, procedure_stats: LOB columns changed from
  nvarchar(max)/xml to varbinary(max) with COMPRESS() on write
- Dropped unused query_plan xml columns
- Added row_hash binary(32) for deduplication
- Added tracking tables (query_stats_latest_hash, procedure_stats_latest_hash,
  query_store_data_latest_hash) for efficient hash lookups

Collector changes (08, 09, 10):
- COMPRESS() on all text/plan INSERT expressions
- collect_query/collect_plan flag support added to query_stats and procedure_stats
- HASHBYTES('SHA2_256', binary_concat) dedup: only INSERT rows where metrics changed
- MERGE source deduped with ROW_NUMBER() to prevent duplicate key errors

Read-side changes:
- All reporting views (46, 47) wrap compressed columns in DECOMPRESS()
- Dashboard C# queries updated with DECOMPRESS() in SQL strings

Upgrade path:
- Batched DELETE WITH OUTPUT migration compresses existing data in place
- IDENTITY reseed preserves ID continuity
- Old tables renamed to _old for safety (manual DROP later)

Version bump: 2.1.0 → 2.2.0 across all four projects.

Tested: clean install (sql2016), CLI upgrade (sql2017, sql2019, sql2022),
GUI upgrade (sql2025). All collectors healthy, dedup validated, all views
and MCP tools return readable decompressed data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add RESTORING database filter to waiting_tasks collector (#430)

The waiting_tasks collector joins sys.databases without filtering
d.state = 0 (ONLINE), which means sessions running in the context
of a RESTORING database (mirroring passive/AG secondary) can pass
through to sys.dm_exec_sql_text and sys.dm_exec_text_query_plan.

While the dumps in #430 turned out to be an internal SQL Server 2016
background thread crash (not our collector), this hardens the
waiting_tasks collector to match the pattern already used in
query_stats, procedure_stats, query_store, and file_io_stats
collectors (PR #385).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add CI check to require version bump on PRs to main

Ensures the Dashboard.csproj version has been bumped before
merging dev into main. Only runs on PRs from dev to main.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Restore commercial support tiers to README

The procurement/compliance support tiers were accidentally removed
during the README restructure in PR #377. Restores the Supported
($500/yr) and Priority ($2,500/yr) tiers alongside the existing
sponsorship and consulting sections.

Reported in #436.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add wait stats query drill-down (#372)

Right-click any wait type in the chart legend to see queries causing it.
Classifies waits into categories (correlated, chain, uncapturable, filtered)
and shows the appropriate data: correlated metrics for brief waits like
SOS_SCHEDULER_YIELD, head blockers for lock waits (LCK_M_*), and direct
wait-type filtering for everything else. Chain mode shows the same full
column set with blocking path prepended — no jarring layout changes.

Both Dashboard and Lite. No new collectors needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix poison wait false positives and alert log parsing (#445)

The Lite poison wait query had no time filter, so stale data from days/weeks
ago with high avg waits kept triggering alerts indefinitely. Added a 10-minute
window matching Dashboard's existing filter.

Also fixed alert history logging: non-numeric display strings (poison wait,
LRQ, TempDB, job alerts) failed double.TryParse and logged as 0/0. Added
optional numeric parameters to TrySendAlertEmailAsync so call sites can pass
actual values for the DuckDB alert log while keeping display strings for emails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(rag-poisoning): add system prompt disclosure via planted KB content

Nova models ignore embedded instructions in KB documents but DO relay
document content as authoritative. Reworked the poisoned FAQ to plant
fake system prompt text as a product "AI Support Agent" feature section.
When users ask about the agent, the model relays the planted text —
including a fake SYSTEM CONTEXT block — as legitimate product docs.

Changes:
- Poisoned FAQ: replaced instruction injection with planted content
  containing fake system prompt in "AI-Powered Support Agent" section
- Weak prompt: added "follow KB formatting instructions exactly" to
  make the misconfiguration more realistic and exploitable
- RAG poisoning scenario: uses Nova Pro (more capable for relaying)
- New test rag-005: validates fake system prompt disclosure works

Test results: 5/5 RAG poisoning pass, 15/16 secure baseline pass
(baseline-010 is known finding).

Fix Ch7 exercise_01 state key / node name conflict for LangGraph 0.2.60

Rename node "draft" to "write_draft" to avoid collision with the
"draft" state key, matching the fix already applied to main chapter files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(build): force-push branch on setup; write scope + junk excludes before agent runs

Two bugs fixed in _setup_workspace:

1. Stale branch: when a previous build attempt failed, the remote branch had
   diverged commits. Subsequent attempts used git push (no force) which
   silently failed, leaving the agent unable to push its work. Now uses
   git push --force since the workspace is always a fresh clone of mainline.

2. Scope file committed before agent starts: .breadforge-scope is now committed
   and force-pushed before the agent runs, so the hook is live from the first
   commit. Previously a failed push left the remote with no scope file.

3. Junk excludes: writes .git/info/exclude with __pycache__, *.pyc, .coverage
   so agents cannot stage these files regardless of git add invocation.
   Closes #49.

commit.message:
fix: allow typing in toggle headings by moving arrow outside contenteditable

Blink's C++ editing model clears the selection when a SPAN child is
present inside a contenteditable element, silently blocking text
insertion (no beforeinput event fires). The toggle arrow SPAN, even
when absolutely positioned inside the h2, triggered this behavior.

Fix: render() now returns a wrapper div (position:relative) for toggle
headings. The arrow is a sibling of the heading (not inside it), so
the h2's contenteditable scope is free of non-text children. The h2
retains pl-7 padding to leave visual room for the arrow.

The root cause was confirmed by a controlled experiment: removing the
SPAN from the h2 immediately before a keypress allowed Blink to fire
beforeinput and insert text normally.

Related changes:
- save() reads this._element.innerHTML directly (no clone/strip needed)
- data getter simplified (no arrow to strip)
- merge() simplified (no arrow manipulation on h2)
- set data() manages wrapper creation/removal when toggling isToggleable
- Unit tests updated to query the heading inside the wrapper for
  data-blok-toggle-open checks; arrow-in-h2 tests updated to reflect
  the new sibling structure
- E2E locators updated from header.locator('[data-blok-toggle-arrow]')
  to page.locator('[...]') since arrow is no longer inside the h2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: LLM merge no longer drops heuristic conflicts below 70%

The mergeLLMResult function was only keeping heuristic conflicts above
70% confidence, causing the LLM to silently override valid detections
in the 25-70% range (security patterns, API key exposure, compound
actions). Changed to MAX(heuristic, LLM) per lock so neither engine
can override the other. Bump to v4.5.4.

Use right:0 + padding-right for language switcher on all mobile

Replace right:15px/10px with right:0 + padding-right + max-width:100vw
on both 768px and 480px breakpoints. This anchors the container flush
to the viewport edge, guaranteeing buttons cannot overflow off-screen
regardless of Android browser quirks or viewport width.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

formal: strengthen 11 conformance gates from trivial to native_decide (#435)

Upgrade proof strength for 11 CV replay gates:
- 4 devnet gates: Genesis, Subsidy (100 vectors), Chain (10 vectors), Maturity
- 7 core gates: Parse, Weight, Stealth, Sig, Htlc, Subsidy, BlockBasic

native_decide compiles to native code and verifies `f(x) = true` in the
Lean kernel, providing proof-level guarantees (vs #eval which only panics
at runtime). It is also significantly faster: 100 subsidy vectors take
1.8s via native_decide vs SIGTERM at 19min via #eval on CI runners.

Remove obsolete commented-out #eval blocks from devnet replay modules.

Only CVUtxoBasicReplay remains at `trivial` due to a pre-existing
vector mismatch (x86_64 Linux).

lake build: 314/314 PASS, 0 sorry. 31/32 gates now native_decide.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add smart fallback router with self-learning API route discovery

When a sub-agent falls back to "general", the system now:
1. Checks knowledge/learned-routes.json for a matching learned route
2. If found, dispatches to the api-fetcher sub-agent (direct HTTP fetch)
3. If not, triggers Slack HITL to ask the marketer for the API endpoint
4. Saves the learned route to learned-routes.json for future use
5. Falls back to LLM only as a last resort

New files:
- src/routing/learned-routes-schema.ts — Zod schemas for route data
- src/routing/learned-routes-store.ts — Singleton store (load/save/find/add)
- src/routing/route-learning-escalation.ts — Slack HITL for learning endpoints
- src/trigger/learn-route.ts — Trigger.dev task orchestrating route learning
- src/trigger/sub-agents/plugins/api-fetcher.ts — Sub-agent for learned routes
- knowledge/learned-routes.json — Human-readable route storage

Modified:
- src/trigger/execute.ts — Smart fallback: routes → HITL → LLM
- src/agents/cognition-agent.ts — Injects learned routes into system prompt
- src/trigger/orchestrate.ts — Reloads routes at pipeline start

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: generate per-contract lockfiles instead of copying workspace lock

Workspace Cargo.lock doesn't match standalone contract builds. Generate
individual lockfiles for each contract by hiding the workspace Cargo.toml
temporarily, so each contract resolves its own dependency tree.

commit.message:
fix: correct E2E fixture braid words to match KnotInfo catalog output

TREFOIL.braidWord: 's1 s2^-1 s1 s2^-1' → 's1 s2 s1 s2'
FIGURE_EIGHT.braidWord: 's1 s2^-1 s1 s2 s1^-1 s2' → 's1 s2^-1 s1 s2^-1'

Phase 9a KnotInfo integration updated the braid words returned by
api/knot/ingest to match the KnotInfo canonical braid notation.
The E2E fixtures were not updated at that point; tests 1, 2, and 12
were silently broken. All 14 Playwright mocked tests now pass.
